# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Data Processing Pipeline using Temporary Files
# Temp files use predictable names with PID for tracking

# Define temp file paths using $$ (process ID) for predictable naming
TEMP_INPUT="/tmp/myapp-input-$$.tmp"
TEMP_PROCESSED="/tmp/myapp-processed-$$.tmp"
TEMP_RESULTS="/tmp/myapp-results-$$.tmp"
TEMP_SUMMARY="/tmp/myapp-summary-$$.tmp"

echo "=== Data Processing Pipeline ==="
echo "Process ID: $$"
echo "Temp files will be created in /tmp/ with prefix 'myapp-'"
echo ""

# Cleanup function to remove temp files on exit
cleanup() {
    echo ""
    echo "=== Cleaning up temporary files ==="
    for tmpfile in "$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_PROCESSED" "$TEMP_RESULTS" "$TEMP_SUMMARY"; do
        if [ -f "$tmpfile" ]; then
            echo "Removing: $tmpfile"
            rm -f "$tmpfile"
        fi
    done
    echo "Cleanup complete."
}

# Register cleanup on EXIT, INT, TERM signals
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 1: Generate raw data and write to input temp file
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 1: Generating raw data ---"
echo "Writing raw data to: $TEMP_INPUT"

# Simulate raw data (e.g., sales records: date, product, quantity, price)
cat > "$TEMP_INPUT" <<EOF
2024-01-15,Widget_A,10,25.50
2024-01-15,Widget_B,5,42.00
2024-01-16,Widget_A,8,25.50
2024-01-16,Widget_C,12,15.75
2024-01-17,Widget_B,3,42.00
2024-01-17,Widget_A,15,25.50
2024-01-17,Widget_C,7,15.75
2024-01-18,Widget_B,9,42.00
2024-01-18,Widget_A,20,25.50
2024-01-18,Widget_C,4,15.75
2024-01-19,Widget_A,6,25.50
2024-01-19,Widget_B,11,42.00
2024-01-19,Widget_C,8,15.75
EOF

RECORD_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_INPUT")
echo "Generated $RECORD_COUNT records."
echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 2: Process/transform data - calculate line totals
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 2: Processing data (calculating line totals) ---"
echo "Reading from: $TEMP_INPUT"
echo "Writing processed data to: $TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Read from input temp file, calculate total per line, write to processed temp file
echo "date,product,quantity,unit_price,line_total" > "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

while IFS=',' read -r date product quantity price; do
    # Calculate line total using awk for floating point arithmetic
    line_total=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $quantity * $price}")
    echo "$date,$product,$quantity,$price,$line_total" >> "$TEMP_PROCESSED"
done < "$TEMP_INPUT"

PROCESSED_COUNT=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | wc -l)
echo "Processed $PROCESSED_COUNT records."
echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 3: Aggregate results - group by product
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 3: Aggregating results (group by product) ---"
echo "Reading from: $TEMP_PROCESSED"
echo "Writing results to: $TEMP_RESULTS"

# Use the same processed temp file path to read and aggregate
echo "product,total_quantity,total_revenue,avg_unit_price" > "$TEMP_RESULTS"

# Extract unique products from the processed temp file (skip header)
products=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | cut -d',' -f2 | sort -u)

for product in $products; do
    # Read from the same TEMP_PROCESSED file multiple times for each product
    total_qty=$(grep ",$product," "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '{sum += $3} END {print sum}')
    total_rev=$(grep ",$product," "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '{sum += $5} END {printf "%.2f", sum}')
    avg_price=$(grep ",$product," "$TEMP_PROCESSED" | awk -F',' '{sum += $4; count++} END {printf "%.2f", sum/count}')
    echo "$product,$total_qty,$total_rev,$avg_price" >> "$TEMP_RESULTS"
done

echo "Aggregated into $(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_RESULTS" | wc -l) product groups."
echo ""

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# STAGE 4: Generate summary report using results temp file
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "--- Stage 4: Generating summary report ---"
echo "Reading from: $TEMP_RESULTS"
echo "Writing summary to: $TEMP_SUMMARY"

{
    echo "============================================"
    echo "       DATA PROCESSING PIPELINE REPORT"
    echo "============================================"
    echo "Generated: $(date)"
    echo "Process ID: $$"
    echo ""
    echo "--- Input Statistics ---"
    echo "Total raw records: $RECORD_COUNT"
    echo "Date range: $(head -1 "$TEMP_INPUT" | cut -d',' -f1) to $(tail -1 "$TEMP_INPUT" | cut -d',' -f1)"
    echo ""
    echo "--- Product Summary ---"
    printf "%-12s %10s %15s %12s\n" "Product" "Qty Sold" "Revenue" "Avg Price"
    printf "%-12s %10s %15s %12s\n" "--------" "--------" "-----------" "---------"

    # Read from TEMP_RESULTS (using same path again)
    while IFS=',' read -r product qty revenue avg_price; do
        printf "%-12s %10s %15s %12s\n" "$product" "$qty" "\$$revenue" "\$$avg_price"
    done < <(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_RESULTS")

    echo ""
    echo "--- Grand Totals ---"
    # Read from TEMP_RESULTS again for grand totals
    grand_qty=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_RESULTS" | awk -F',' '{sum += $2} END {print sum}')
    grand_rev=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_RESULTS" | awk -F',' '{sum += $3} END {printf "%.2f", sum}')
    echo "Total quantity sold: $grand_qty"
    echo "Total revenue: \$$grand_rev"
    echo ""

    # Re-read TEMP_RESULTS to find top product
    top_product=$(tail -n +2 "$TEMP_RESULTS" | sort -t',' -k3 -rn | head -1)
    top_name=$(echo "$top_product" | cut -d',' -f1)
    top_rev=$(echo "$top_product" | cut -d',' -f3)
    echo "Top product by revenue: $top_name (\$$top_rev)"
    echo ""
    echo "--- Temp Files Used ---"
    echo "  Input:     $TEMP_INPUT ($(stat -c%s "$TEMP_INPUT" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$TEMP_INPUT" 2>/dev/null) bytes)"
    echo "  Processed: $TEMP_PROCESSED ($(stat -c%s "$TEMP_PROCESSED" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$TEMP_PROCESSED" 2>/dev/null) bytes)"
    echo "  Results:   $TEMP_RESULTS ($(stat -c%s "$TEMP_RESULTS" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$TEMP_RESULTS" 2>/dev/null) bytes)"
    echo "  Summary:   $TEMP_SUMMARY"
    echo ""
    echo "============================================"
    echo "            END OF REPORT"
    echo "============================================"
} > "$TEMP_SUMMARY"

echo ""
echo "=== Final Report ==="
echo ""

# Read and display the summary from the temp file
cat "$TEMP_SUMMARY"

echo ""
echo "=== Pipeline Complete ==="
echo "All temp files stored in /tmp/ with pattern: myapp-*-$$.tmp"
echo "Listing temp files:"
ls -la /tmp/myapp-*-$$.tmp 2>/dev/null

# Cleanup will be triggered by the EXIT trap
